name: Downward-Facing Dog
assists: push downward on the lumbar zone
description: Adho Mukha Svanasana (Downward-Facing Dog) gets its name from Sanskrit: Adho (down), Mukha (face), Svana (dog), and Asana (pose). It mimics the deep, natural stretch of a waking dog. The pose stretches your legs, builds upper-body strength, and acts as an active resting position
notes: Draft entry from seed photo import; review and correct before use in a real class.
